task_updates.executor_notes: Verified `src/tools/video-editor/lib/pinned-group-projection.ts` provides the requested pure projection/group helpers. `projectPinnedShotGroups` rewrites clip timing/track from `group.start + child.offset`, `buildGroupFromConfigClips` sorts source clips by `clip.at` and derives `start = max(0, min(at))`, and every writer funnels through `buildGroupFromChildren`, which normalizes children before returning.

Confirmed the clone helpers in `src/tools/video-editor/lib/migrate.ts`, `src/tools/video-editor/lib/timeline-data.ts`, and `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/usePinnedShotGroups.ts` all deep-copy `start`, `clipIds`, `children`, and `imageClipSnapshot.meta`. Grep over `src/tools/video-editor/` did not reveal another dedicated clone helper that still drops the new fields.

Verified `repairConfig` now performs an idempotent legacy migration for pinned groups missing `start` or `children`. The rebuild path uses `buildGroupFromConfigClips`, which drops missing `clipIds`, orders remaining clips by start time, derives `start` from the minimum clip position with `start >= 0`, and no-ops on subsequent runs once both fields exist.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Confirmed `pinned-group-projection.ts` exports both `buildGroupFromChildren` and `buildGroupFromConfigClips`, and every mutating helper reaches `normalizeChildren` through `buildGroupFromChildren`.
Confirmed all three requested clone helpers deep-copy both `start` and `children`; grep did not find another dedicated clone helper in `src/tools/video-editor/` still omitting them.
Confirmed the legacy migration is idempotent, drops missing clip ids by rebuilding from live clips, and clamps `start >= 0` through `buildGroupFromConfigClips`.
